prompt: Gradient descent visualized as a traveler descending a mountain, in authentic Japanese ukiyo-e woodblock print style (Edo period, 1603–1868). Composition: diagonal slope dominating the frame, guiding the eye downward. A lone traveler follows a zig-zag path toward a valley, representing iterative optimization steps. Visual grammar: flat, unshaded color regions, bold black contour outlines, limited palette of indigo blue, muted green, beige, off-white, stylized clouds, visible woodblock texture. Symbolism: mountain = loss function, path = iterations, valley = global minimum. No modern elements, no realism, no gradients, no 3D shading.
tradition_approach: Used flat colors, bold outlines, diagonal composition, minimal shading, and symbolic landscape to match ukiyo-e grammar while embedding optimization path.
